title: The Boy Lost and Found
subtitle: Jesus, twelve years old, remains behind in the Temple among the doctors

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the road fall away.
He who is the Wisdom of the Father waits quietly in the Temple.
Enter the silence where he is found.
gospel: The boy Jesus grew, and was strengthened,
full of wisdom; and the grace of God was in him.
And his parents went every year to Jerusalem,
on the solemn day of the Passover.
And when he was twelve years old,
they went up to Jerusalem, according to the custom of the feast.
And when the days were ended, they returned;
but the boy Jesus remained in Jerusalem,
and his parents knew it not.
After three days they found him in the Temple,
sitting in the midst of the doctors,
hearing them, and asking them questions.
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the boy Jesus, wearied even now by the long road,
going to honor his heavenly Father at his feasts;
the Lord of the Law, humbly keeping the Law.
He kept the very Law he gave,
to show us, who are mere men,
that whatever God commands must be kept in all things.
Behold him, that you may follow the path of his human life,
if you delight to gaze upon the glory of his Godhead.
Behold him among the doctors, with a calm face,
and how, as though unknowing, he questions them and listens;
which he does out of humility,
and lest they be ashamed at his marvelous answers.
consider: He remained behind, not by chance,
not by his parents' neglect or forgetting,
but by his own will and ordaining:
that from boyhood he might show his zeal for the things of the spirit.
As he had rendered to his parents what was owed,
going up with them as a man among men to offer sacrifice to God,
so now he would render to the Father what was due,
From boyhood men ought to grow accustomed to divine things.
So that the brother of such great devotion to the feasts of Christ
prepared himself beforehand by fasting, prayer, and chastisements,
and as long as the feast lasted, gave himself to meditation and prayer.
And consider this for yourself: he who would cleave to God
must not dwell among his kinsmen,
but rather withdraw himself from them.
His twelve years were a figure of the twelve Apostles,
through whom his divinity and humanity were to be proclaimed
through the whole round of the world.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, Son of the living God,
who for three days were sought by your grieving parents,
and at last were found in the Temple:
grant to me, a wretch, to desire you,
by loving to redeem my evils,
and, once redeemed, to do them no more.
You who give freely to him that asks,
are found by him that seeks,
are opened to him that knocks:
deny not to me, the least, what you promise to all.
And you who, returning to Nazareth at your parents' will,
and made subject to them, showed the pattern of obedience:
grant to me, hardened, to break my own will,
that I may be subject to you,
and, for your sake, to every human creature. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Da mihi misero te desiderare, desiderando quærere, quærendo invenire, inveniendo amare, amando mala mea redimere, redempta non iterare.
latinJewel.english: Grant to me, a wretch, to desire you; by desiring, to seek you; by seeking, to find you; by finding, to love you; by loving, to redeem my evils; and, once redeemed, to do them no more.
